summary:
Only include essential information from Agent1’s response related directly to answering whether raspberries improve postprandial glucose and/or inflammation in T2D patients. Avoid adding external knowledge or interpretation beyond what was explicitly stated by the agent.

The provided evidence does not show that raspberry consumption improves postprandial glucose or reduces acute/chronic inflammation in adults with Type 2 Diabetes due to a complete lack of direct clinical studies addressing these endpoints. Though raspberries contain beneficial phytochemicals like polyphenols, no supported trial findings link them to measurable changes in blood sugar responses or inflammatory markers among diabetic subjects.
